The Linux kernel before 4.1.11 does not properly handle certain invalid memory accesses in the drm_gem_object_lookup_user_ioctl function in drivers/gpu/drm/gem/gem.c, which allows local users to gain privileges or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted ioctl call.

libimageworsener.a in ImageWorsener before 1.3.1 has *left shift cannot be represented in type int* undefined behavior issues, which might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (application crash) or possibly have unspecified other impact via a crafted image, related to imagew-bmp.c and imagew-util.c.

The Android Debug Bridge (ADB) in Android before 4.4.4 does not properly handle the "wait-for-device" command, which allows attackers to bypass intended access restrictions, aka internal bug 26313234.

The Linux kernel before 4.1.11 does not properly handle a certain type of invalid pointer dereference, which allows local users to gain privileges or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted application, aka a "use-after-free" issue.
The Android kernel through 4.4.4 does not properly handle a certain type of invalid memory access, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka internal bug 26403620.
The Android kernel through 4.4.4 does not properly handle a certain type of USB device, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, aka internal bug 26403620.

The Linux kernel before 4.1.11 does not properly handle a certain type of crafted USB device request, which allows local users to gain privileges or cause a denial of service (memory corruption) via a crafted application, aka internal bug 2014119531.
The Android kernel through 4.4.4 does not properly handle a certain type of USB device, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted USB device, aka internal bug 26403620.
